articles.summary: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond targeted ads. "Businesses often share information about people's activity on their sites with us to make ads more relevant," Meta said in a statement. "We already use this data - like games you play

keypoints: Meta expands business data usage beyond ad targeting
Off-site activity now feeds AI personalization and content ranking

analysis.title: Scope Expansion Beyond Ads
analysis.desc: Meta shifts from using business data solely for targeted advertising to incorporating it into feed algorithms and AI chatbot responses.

analysis.title: Data Collection Scope Widens
analysis.desc: Information about user activity on third-party sites now flows into Meta's AI personalization systems, not just ad relevance.

analysis.title: Privacy Implications Unclear
analysis.desc: Users may not realize their cross-site activity influences both content they see and AI responses they receive from Meta.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Meta Weaponizes Off-Site Data for AI Personalization Beyond Ads
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your activity on other websites is now feeding Meta's AI — and you probably didn't know it.
ai_summary.action_today: Meta → Settings → Apps and Websites → Review and remove connected apps you don't use

articles.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
articles.summary: Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out of a maximum of 10.0. "A vulnerability allowing remote code execution (RCE) on the Backup Server by an authenticated domain user," Veeam said in a Tuesday advisory. It

keypoints: Veeam RCE vulnerability enables authenticated domain user code execution
Critical CVSS 9.4 flaw requires immediate patching of backup servers

analysis.title: Critical severity threat
analysis.desc: CVE-2026-44963 allows authenticated domain users remote code execution on Veeam Backup Server with maximum CVSS severity.

analysis.title: Attack vector scope
analysis.desc: Vulnerability requires domain user authentication, limiting exposure to internal threats but posing significant enterprise risk.

analysis.title: Mitigation status
analysis.desc: Veeam released patches Tuesday. Organizations must prioritize updates for backup infrastructure protecting critical business data.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Veeam Backup RCE Flaw — CVSS 9.4 Demands Immediate Patching
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your backup server could be compromised by anyone with domain credentials — patch immediately or assume breach.
ai_summary.action_today: Veeam Backup Console → Check for Updates → Download and install patches now

articles.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
articles.summary: Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject an information stealer into the code. "Our priority is to protect customers and the broader ecosystem," a Microsoft spokesperson told The Hacker News via email. "We temporarily removed some

keypoints: 73 Microsoft open-source projects compromised with information stealer
GitHub repositories temporarily offline during Miasma security incident

analysis.title: Supply Chain Attack Scope
analysis.desc: Microsoft's 73 compromised open-source projects represent significant supply chain risk affecting downstream developers using affected code.

analysis.title: Incident Response Strategy
analysis.desc: Microsoft's selective repository restoration balances ecosystem protection with investigation continuity, keeping higher-risk projects offline pending forensic completion.

analysis.title: Information Stealer Threat
analysis.desc: Injected malware targets developer credentials and sensitive data, threatening authentication tokens and private keys across affected development environments.

ai_summary.suggested_title: GitHub Supply Chain Attack: 73 Microsoft Repos Compromised
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your development dependencies may already contain stolen credential harvesters — audit your GitHub activity logs now.
